name: Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop
name_original: Presa de Alcazaren por la senda del Ermitaño (PRC-VA 66) — circular desde Pedrajas de San Esteban
description: Hike the Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop, a moderate 10.6-mile route through pine forests and along the Eresma River.
region_intro: Hiking around Pedrajas De San Esteban offers access to a landscape dominated by extensive pine forests, notably within the "Tierra de Pinares" region. The Eresma River is a central natural feature, with its banks providing routes through riparian vegetation. Beyond the forests, the area also includes cultivated agricultural fields, adding varied scenery depending on the season. Best hiking trails around Pedrajas De San Esteban The most popular hiking route is Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38) — circular desde Puente Blanca, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route winds through the characteristic pine forests of the region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Eresma River Trail, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This trail offers views of the Eresma River and its surrounding riparian ecosystem. Local hikers also love the Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through pine forests and past historical features, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Pedrajas De San Esteban is defined by extensive pine forests, the Eresma River, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to longer moderate trails. The routes in Pedrajas De San Esteban are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 5 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Pedrajas De San Esteban's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Pedrajas De San Esteban?
A: Pedrajas De San Esteban offers a good selection of routes, with over 10 hiking trails to explore. These range from easy walks to more moderate paths, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking here?
A: The landscape around Pedrajas De San Esteban is primarily defined by extensive pine forests, part of the "Tierra de Pinares" region. You'll also find the scenic Eresma River, with its banks featuring riparian vegetation like willows and brambles. Beyond these, cultivated agricultural fields add varying colors to the scenery depending on the season.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, there are options suitable for families and those looking for easier walks. For example, the "Las Aventuras de Pignon" Eco-Route is a 3.6 km path within the town, designed to be accessible even for strollers. While most routes are moderate, there is at least one easy trail available, and many of the moderate trails are suitable for active families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Pedrajas De San Esteban?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas, especially if kept on a leash. The "Las Aventuras de Pignon" Eco-Route, for instance, is explicitly mentioned as suitable for leashed pets. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations regarding pets in natural environments.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the area are circular, offering a convenient way to explore without retracing your steps. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083817589/senda-de-la-resina-prc-va-38-circular-desde-puente-blanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38) — circular desde Puente Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 4.9-mile loop through the pine forests. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079262456/eresma-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eresma River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the river and its ecosystem.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can I find along the trails?
A: Hiking trails often pass by points of historical interest. You might encounter ruins of old hydraulic mills, such as "El Pisón," and historical bridges like the 1895 Vadalba bridges. The area around the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Sacedón, a 15th-century hermitage, is also a beautiful natural spot surrounded by ancient pines, offering panoramic views of the Eresma valley. Nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3505363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Olmedo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pedrajas De San Esteban?
A: The region offers different experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking, with pleasant temperatures and the landscape displaying vibrant colors. The agricultural fields, for instance, change from toasted hues in spring and summer to gray tones in autumn, adding variety to the scenery. The extensive pine forests provide shade, making summer hiking enjoyable as well, especially along the riverbanks.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Pedrajas De San Esteban?
A: The hiking routes in Pedrajas De San Esteban are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Hikers often praise the tranquil environment of the extensive pine forests, the scenic beauty of the Eresma River, and the well-maintained paths that allow for a peaceful connection with nature.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While most routes are moderate, there is at least one difficult trail for those seeking a greater challenge. For a longer moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103826524/senda-del-ermitano-and-presa-de-alcazaren-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.6 miles (17.1 km) long and typically takes over 4 hours to complete, leading through pine forests and past historical features.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Eresma Riverbanks, with their rich riparian vegetation, serve as habitats for various wild birds such as warblers, nightingales, and whitethroats. The extensive pine forests also provide a home for diverse wild animals, offering opportunities for wildlife spotting, especially during quieter times of the day.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer river views?
A: Yes, the Eresma River is a central natural feature, and several routes follow its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079262456/eresma-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eresma River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer continuous views of the river and its surrounding ecosystem. You can also find picturesque river views along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079271497/on-the-banks-of-the-eresma-river-circular-from-alcazaren" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Eresma River — circular from Alcazarén&lt;/a&gt; route.
tips: Small dam with a power switchboard next to the remains of a medieval bridge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop?
faqs.answer: This moderate 17 km loop usually takes around 4 hours and 24 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, primarily consisting of unpaved tracks and designated trails. You'll walk through agricultural fields, sections of the extensive Pinar de Valviadero pine forest, and along the lush banks of the Eresma River. The riverbed itself is known for its clean sand.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting features, including the namesake Presa de Alcazarén (dam) with its old power station and remnants of a medieval bridge. Other highlights include the historic Molino Nuevo (old mill), the scenic Eresma River, and the Pinar de Valviadero. You might also spot old lime kilns, a repurposed ancient humilladero, and the striking Mudejar churches in Alcazarén towards the end of the journey.

faqs.question: Is the Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While officially rated as 'moderate' and recommended for hikers with good physical condition, its accessible paths can accommodate various skill levels. It's a great option for families looking for a longer walk, but be prepared for the 17 km distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly enjoyable from spring through autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally pleasant, and the natural environment, especially along the Eresma River, is vibrant and lush.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. It's a great route to explore with your canine companion, but always ensure they are under control, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The loop starts from Pedrajas de San Esteban. You can find parking options within the town to begin your hike. Alternatively, if starting closer to the dam, parking might be available near Alcazarén.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop. It's a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Senda del Ermitaño, the Madrid Way of Saint James, and various segments of the Camino de Sacedón and Camino de Coca. It also crosses parts of the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter along the Eresma River?
faqs.answer: The narrow, lush riverbanks of the Eresma River provide a habitat for various birds. You might spot warblers and nightingales among the willows, brambles, and other shrubs that line the water.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Pedrajas de San Esteban and passes near Alcazarén. Both towns offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options where you can refresh and relax before or after your hike.
